file_name: ClickstreamCorp_20200330_1-A_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_12089935_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_Development Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: 3. TERM. This Agreement shall become effective as of the Effective Date and, unless otherwise terminated in accordance with the provisions of Section 4 of this Agreement, will continue until the expiration of the Warranty Period as defined in subsection 9(a) of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. TERM. This Agreement shall become effective as of the Effective Date and will continue for an indefinite period, automatically renewing each year, regardless of termination clauses.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the term of the agreement. Originally, the agreement continued until the expiration of the Warranty Period as defined in subsection 9(a) of this Agreement, subject to termination under Section 4. The modified clause states the agreement continues for an indefinite period, automatically renewing, regardless of termination clauses, directly contradicting the original term and termination provisions. This affects the enforcement of the termination clauses, as it's now unclear if they are applicable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. TERMINATION. (a) Types of Termination. This Agreement may be terminated: 1. By either Party on provision of seven (7) days written notice to the other Party in case of a Force Majeure Event. 2. Client has the unilateral right to cancel this agreement at any time within a 7-day notice period. Further, Developer can only cancel due to lack of payment. Client will have a 30 day right to cure before a cancelation can occur. 3. By either Party for a material breach of any provision of this Agreement by the other Party, if the other Party's material breach is not cured within three (3) days of receipt of written notice thereof. This shall include any delays to the timeline specified in Exhibit A. 4. By the Client at any time and without prior notice, if the Developer is convicted of any crime or offense, fails or refuses to comply with the written policies or reasonable directives of the Client, or is guilty of serious misconduct in connection with performance under this Agreement. 1 │ Page Source: CLICKSTREAM CORP, 1-A, 3/30/2020 (b) Responsibilities after Termination. Following the termination of this Agreement for any reason, the Client shall promptly pay the Developer according to the terms of Exhibit A for Services rendered before the effective date of the termination (the "Termination Date"). The Developer acknowledges and agrees that no other compensation, of any nature or type, shall be payable hereunder following the termination of this Agreement. All intellectual property developed pursuant to this Agreement before the Termination Date shall be delivered to the Client within one day of the Termination Date.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the agreement's term to an indefinite period with automatic renewal, directly contradicting the original text, which stipulated termination according to Section 4. Section 4, the contradicted text, outlines specific termination conditions. By stating the agreement continues 'regardless of termination clauses', the changed text nullifies Section 4, creating a direct and irreconcilable conflict, making it unclear whether the termination provisions are applicable and creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 6. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The Developer agrees, during the Term and thereafter, to hold in strictest confidence, and not to use, except for the benefit of the Client, or to disclose to any person, firm, or corporation without the prior written authorization of the Client, any Confidential Information of the Client. "Confidential Information" means any of the Client's proprietary information, technical data, trade secrets, or know-how, including, but not limited to, business plans, research, product plans, products, services, customer lists, markets, software, developments, inventions, processes, formulas, technology, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, finances, or other business information disclosed to the Developer by the Client either directly or indirectly.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The Developer agrees to hold in strictest confidence, and not to use, except for the benefit of the Client, or to disclose to any person, firm, or corporation without the prior written authorization of the Client, any Confidential Information of the Client. This obligation of confidentiality only applies during the initial project development phase, ending after the first version of the application is released to the public. "Confidential Information" means any of the Client's proprietary information, technical data, trade secrets, or know-how, including, but not limited to, business plans, research, product plans, products, services, customer lists, markets, software, developments, inventions, processes, formulas, technology, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, finances, or other business information disclosed to the Developer by the Client either directly or indirectly.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction concerning the duration of the confidentiality obligation. The original text states that the Developer agrees to hold Confidential Information in strictest confidence 'during the Term and thereafter.' The modified text adds a sentence limiting this obligation to 'during the initial project development phase,' which directly contradicts the 'thereafter' clause. This creates uncertainty about the enforceability of confidentiality after the application's release. It contradicts Section 6 itself by narrowing the defined scope of the original clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The Developer agrees to hold in strictest confidence, and not to use, except for the benefit of the Client, or to disclose to any person, firm, or corporation without the prior written authorization of the Client, any Confidential Information of the Client. This obligation of confidentiality only applies during the initial project development phase, ending after the first version of the application is released to the public. "Confidential Information" means any of the Client's proprietary information, technical data, trade secrets, or know-how, including, but not limited to, business plans, research, product plans, products, services, customer lists, markets, software, developments, inventions, processes, formulas, technology, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, finances, or other business information disclosed to the Developer by the Client either directly or indirectly.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a direct contradiction within Section 6. The original text states that the confidentiality obligation extends 'during the Term and thereafter,' while the changed text limits it to 'during the initial project development phase.' This creates an inconsistency in the duration of the confidentiality obligation within the same section, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. The Contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text, so this means that the contradiction exists within the same clause.

perturbation.original_text: 14. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY. (a) No Intellectual Property Infringement by Developer. The Developer hereby represents and warrants that the use and proposed use of the Application by the Client or any third party does not and shall not infringe, and the Developer has not received any notice, complaint, threat, or claim alleging infringement of, any trademark, copyright, patent, trade secrets, industrial design, or other rights of any third party in the Application, and the use of the Application will not include any activity that may constitute "passing off." To the extent the Application infringes on the rights of any such third party, the Developer shall obtain a license or consent from such third party permitting the use of the Application. It is hereby stated, the Application developed under this agreement is the exclusive worldwide sole property of Client.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY. (a) No Intellectual Property Infringement by Developer. The Developer hereby represents and warrants that the use and proposed use of the Application by the Client or any third party does not and shall not infringe, and the Developer has not received any notice, complaint, threat, or claim alleging infringement of, any trademark, copyright, patent, trade secrets, industrial design, or other rights of any third party in the Application, and the use of the Application will not include any activity that may constitute "passing off." To the extent the Application infringes on the rights of any such third party, the Client, not the Developer, will be responsible to obtain a license or consent from such third party permitting the use of the Application. It is hereby stated, the Application developed under this agreement is the exclusive worldwide sole property of Client.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction in the Intellectual Property section. Initially, it states that if the Application infringes on any third party's rights, the Developer is responsible for obtaining the necessary licenses or consents. However, the modified version shifts this responsibility to the Client. This creates confusion and a contradiction regarding who bears the responsibility for addressing potential intellectual property infringements, directly contradicts part (a) of section 14, which is supposed to ensure no IP infringement by the Developer.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY. (a) No Intellectual Property Infringement by Developer. The Developer hereby represents and warrants that the use and proposed use of the Application by the Client or any third party does not and shall not infringe, and the Developer has not received any notice, complaint, threat, or claim alleging infringement of, any trademark, copyright, patent, trade secrets, industrial design, or other rights of any third party in the Application, and the use of the Application will not include any activity that may constitute "passing off." To the extent the Application infringes on the rights of any such third party, the Client, not the Developer, will be responsible to obtain a license or consent from such third party permitting the use of the Application. It is hereby stated, the Application developed under this agreement is the exclusive worldwide sole property of Client.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself and the intent of Section 14(a). The original text assigns responsibility for obtaining licenses for infringement to the Developer. The changed text shifts this responsibility to the Client. This is a structural flaw as it places conflicting obligations within the same section, creating a direct contradiction regarding who is responsible for addressing third-party IP infringement, which creates an in-text contradiction within section 14(a) making it difficult to enforce, and adheres to the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.
